channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 IDF Forces Deploy 10 Km Deep Into Lebanese Territory After Crossing Litani River
item.description: Israeli forces have advanced deep into southern Lebanon, crossing the Litani River and establishing positions 10 kilometers inside Lebanese territory amid ongoing tensions with Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇸🇪🇷🇺 Sweden Detains Two Suspects Over Allegedly Aiding High-Tech Shipments to Russia
item.description: Swedish authorities have detained two people on suspicion of violating international sanctions by helping Russia procure advanced products, marking an escalation in enforcement of export controls.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇷🇺 EU Rejects Putin's Proposal for Ex-German Leader Schroeder in Future Security Talks
item.description: EU ministers dismissed Russia's suggestion that former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder play a role in future security discussions, citing his close relationship with Putin.

item.title: ⛪🇮🇱 Israeli Soldiers Jailed for Desecration of Virgin Mary Statue in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli military jails soldiers after a viral photo showed an Israeli soldier posing with a Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon, holding a cigarette up to its mouth in a desecration incident.

item.title: 🇺🇬🌍 Uganda's Museveni, 81, Sworn In for New Term After 40 Years in Power
item.description: Ugandan President Yoweri Museveni, 81, was sworn in for another five-year term after 40 years at the helm, with his son and presumptive heir emerging as de facto ruler.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Prime Minister Starmer Refuses to Resign Amid Growing Leadership Crisis
item.description: British PM Keir Starmer faces mounting pressure to step down but says he will not resign, in what media describe as the most serious political crisis of his tenure.

item.title: 🔒 Google Thwarts Zero-Day Attack Developed With AI
item.description: Google says it stopped a sophisticated zero-day hack that was likely developed with AI assistance, marking one of the first confirmed cases of AI-assisted cyberattacks in the wild.

item.title: 🎓 Canvas Learning Platform Reaches Deal With Hackers to Prevent Massive Data Leak
item.description: Instructure, which owns the Canvas learning platform used by thousands of schools, paid hackers to prevent the release of 3.65TB of student data after a massive breach.

item.title: 📺 Texas Sues Netflix for Spying on Users Including Children
item.description: Texas filed a lawsuit against Netflix alleging the streaming giant illegally collected data on users including children, amid growing scrutiny of platform surveillance practices.

item.title: 🌾 Former World Bank Chief Says China Should Stop Hoarding Food and Fertilizer
item.description: David Malpass criticized China strategic stockpiling of food and fertilizer, saying its claim to developing nation status was no longer credible.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 California Mayor Resigns After Pleading Guilty to Acting as Chinese Agent
item.description: Eileen Wang, mayor of Arcadia, California, agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government. She was accused of promoting pro-Beijing propaganda without notifying US authorities, including content denying Uyghur genocide.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇰🇪🌍 France Announces $27 Billion Investment Package at Africa Summit in Kenya
item.description: President Macron co-hosted the Africa Forward Summit in English-speaking Kenya for the first time, marking a shift away from traditional Francophone Africa ties. The move comes amid rising anti-French sentiment in former colonies.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇬🇱 US in Secret Talks to Open Three New Military Bases in Greenland
item.description: The US is negotiating with Denmark to establish three new military bases in southern Greenland, focused on surveillance of Russian and Chinese activity in the GIUK Gap. The talks aim to resolve tensions after Trump threatened to seize the territory.

item.title: 🇧🇴⚖️ Bolivia Issues Arrest Warrant for Former President Evo Morales
item.description: A Bolivian judge reissued an arrest warrant for ex-President Evo Morales after he failed to appear for his trial on charges of trafficking a minor. Morales is accused of fathering a child with a 15-year-old girl while in office.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea Cautious on Blaming Iran for Ship Strike Ahead of US-China Summit
item.description: South Korea has footage of the May 4 attack on the HMM Namu vessel but analysts say Seoul is biding its time, hoping the situation will settle as Trump and Xi prepare to meet in Beijing.

item.title: 🤖 Unitree Debuts $574K Transforming Mecha Robot in China
item.description: Chinese robotics company Unitree has unveiled a $574,000 GD01 mecha robot that transforms from two legs to four, expanding its portfolio as Chinese manufacturers significantly outpace US rivals in robotics.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Deepens AI Footprint at Conference Despite US Tensions
item.description: Alibaba, ByteDance and other Chinese tech giants topped paper submissions at the NeurIPS conference, quelling concerns that geopolitical tensions would impact participation in global AI research.

item.title: ✈️ European Airlines Adding China Flights Despite Russian Airspace Barrier
item.description: Demand for China-Europe flights is surging as airlines avoid flying over Iranian airspace and divert tourists away from Southeast Asia, with carriers expanding routes despite Russian airspace restrictions.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇮🇱 EU imposes sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
item.description: The European Union agreed to sanction Israeli settlers accused of violence against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank. Israel called the move "arbitrary" while the EU foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences."

item.title: 🇵🇰🇦🇫 Pakistan airstrike killed 269 Afghans at rehab centre, families demand answers
item.description: A March 16 Pakistani airstrike on what officials called a militant target killed 269 Afghans at a rehabilitation centre. The UN says the death toll may be higher, with calls mounting to investigate the strike as a war crime.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Duterte drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest in Philippines
item.description: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, former national police chief under ex-President Duterte, has sought refuge in the Philippine senate to avoid arrest by the International Criminal Court over his role in the deadly drug war.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky's former chief of staff Yermak charged in major corruption probe
item.description: Ukraine's anti-corruption authorities have charged Andriy Yermak, former head of the presidential administration and Zelensky's closest aide, with corruption offenses. The case involves state-owned nuclear energy company Energoatom and marks the highest-level corruption investigation since the war began.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE secretly carried out strikes on Iranian oil facilities in April
item.description: The UAE covertly attacked Iranian oil infrastructure on Lavan Island in the Persian Gulf in early April, according to US media reports. The UAE had been a target of Iranian retaliation but had not publicly acknowledged conducting offensive operations against Iran.

item.title: 🇵🇰 US says Pakistan allowed Iran to park military aircraft on its airbases
item.description: A US report claims Pakistan permitted Iran to station military aircraft on Pakistani territory during the conflict. The allegation could strain US-Pakistan relations and raises questions about regional alignments during the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea amid Hormuz crisis
item.description: France has deployed its aircraft carrier to the Red Sea, positioning it for potential operations near the Strait of Hormuz as the US-Iran conflict continues. The move underscores European concerns about energy security and freedom of navigation.
